description: Lock Manual Boat Propulsion to source-backed Egyptian oar/manual boat propulsion evidence, not an unsupported generic prehistoric paddle date or sail propulsion.

checks.reason: The launch-quality date is anchored to source-backed Naqada II / fourth-millennium BCE Egyptian oar-and-boat evidence rather than the old Ancient default placeholder.
Manual propulsion is an operating method for a watercraft platform, not merely a loose historical predecessor.
Woodworking underlies shaped wooden oars, paddles, and poles but is not a one-to-one hard requirement for every manual-propulsion implement.
The broad manual-propulsion node must not depend on the narrower Mesolithic dugout-paddle artifact node.
